basics.name: Chris (Haolin) Qu
basics.label: Software Engineer
basics.summary: Full-stack and backend engineer with 2+ years of experience designing and scaling distributed systems and data-driven applications using Java, Python, React, and cloud technologies.

work.name: Daxwell
work.summary: Architected and scaled a distributed ERP/CRM platform on AWS EKS.
highlights: Architected and scaled a **distributed ERP/CRM platform** on **AWS EKS**, owning end-to-end design across microservices, data pipelines, and infrastructure.
Designed a **high-consistency transactional engine** handling dynamic pricing, contract lifecycle management, and state transitions across **distributed services**.
Built real-time **supply-demand orchestration** integrating orders, procurement, and inventory using **event-driven architecture (Kafka)**.
Improved platform scalability through containerized microservices **(Spring Boot, GraphQL, RESTful, Redis, PostgreSQL)**, reducing latency by 60%.
Established secure **service-to-service authentication (Auth0** OIDC, **RBAC)** and production-grade observability **(CI/CD, metrics, tracing, logging)**.
Designed a **streaming and integration layer** linking **IoT (Project44)**, **logistics (OTM)**, **CRM (Salesforce)**, and ERP data through **Kafka, Flink** into **Databricks**.

work.name: Xiaomi Technology
highlights: Developed flash sale functionality for the 618 Shopping Festival, leveraging **Nginx** for load balancing and **Redis** for caching.
Implemented **Apache Kafka** and multithreading for efficient asynchronous processing.
Contributed to a high-traffic WeChat account, using **Apache Kafka** to create a dead-letter queue for message notifications.
Completed backend requirements for H5 pages, developed real-time Olympic medal tally feature with Spring Boot.

work.name: WeBank
highlights: Developed and optimized a high-performance data system, utilizing **Redis** for efficient data storage and retrieval of financial transactions.
Implemented backend services using **RESTful API**, **Spring framework**, **Spring Boot** to enhance system efficiency.
Utilized **Apache Kafka** as a message queue to enable asynchronous processing and load balancing.
Implemented a microservices architecture, using **Spring Cloud** to ensure stability and consistency of distributed system.

courses: Rapid Prototype Development and Creative Programming
